title: clinopodium graveolens subsp. rotundifolium
intro: Clinopodium graveolens subsp. rotundifolium, also known as round-leaved wild basil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. The plant is native to the Mediterranean region and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Clinopodium graveolens subsp. rotundifolium include relief from digestive issues, reduction of inflammation, and antimicrobial protection, making it a potential treatment for various ailments. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
